name: Pontecesures — circular of the Rio Ulla Rio Sar and Padrón
name_original: Pontecesures — circular del Rio Ulla Rio Sar y Padrón
region_intro: Jogging routes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza traverse a Special Zone of Conservation in Galicia, characterized by the Ulla River and its tributaries, including the Deza River. The landscape features diverse riparian forests, green hills, and the estuary in the Ría de Arousa. Terrain varies from easily accessible paths along riverbanks to more challenging routes with elevation gains, such as those near the Toxa River Waterfall. Best jogging routes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza The most popular jogging route is Pontecesures — circular of the Rio Ulla Rio Sar and Padrón, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete. This route offers views along the Ulla and Sar rivers. Another top favourite among local runners is Fervenza do Toxa – Carboeiro Monastery loop from SAC Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza, a difficult 13.6 miles (21.9 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes by the Toxa River Waterfall and the Monastery of Carboeiro. Local runners also love the Petróglifos de Vilar loop from Bertamiráns, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza is defined by river corridors, dense riparian forests, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more demanding trails with elevation. The routes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 140 trails available. These range from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The terrain in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza is quite varied. You'll find easily accessible paths along riverbanks, meandering through lush riparian forests, as well as more moderate and difficult routes that include elevation gains of up to 1,000 feet. This diversity ensures options for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Sar-Ulla Recreation Park loop from Nestlé&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5 km (2.8 miles) trail with minimal elevation, ideal for a gentle jog along the river.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by natural landmarks like waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! A significant natural highlight is the Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall, one of Galicia's most spectacular. You can experience this on the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24607224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa – Carboeiro Monastery loop from SAC Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes the historic Monastery of Carboeiro.

Q: What historical or cultural sights can I see while running in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: Many routes combine natural beauty with cultural exploration. You can jog past historical bridges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1859587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontevea Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Carboeiro&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features traditional Galician manor houses ('pazos') and is part of the Camino de Santiago routes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984974641/pontecesures-circular-of-the-rio-ulla-rio-sar-and-padron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontecesures — circular of the Rio Ulla Rio Sar and Padrón&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views along the Ulla and Sar rivers.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the riparian forests, and the combination of natural beauty with historical elements. The variety of trails for different abilities is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: For families, the easier, flatter paths along the riverbanks are ideal. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Sar-Ulla Recreation Park loop from Nestlé&lt;/a&gt; offer a pleasant and accessible environment for a family jog, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural surroundings.

Q: Is it possible to find parking near the running trails?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many popular starting points for routes, especially those near towns or recreational areas, offer designated parking. It's always a good idea to check the route details on komoot for specific parking information before you go.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions for running, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons ideal, while winter offers a serene, often misty landscape, though some paths might be muddier.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Ría de Arousa estuary?
A: Yes, as the Ulla River flows towards the Ría de Arousa, some routes will bring you closer to its estuary. This area offers a distinct natural environment and beautiful views where the river meets the sea, providing a different scenic backdrop for your run.

Q: Can I combine my run with exploring other attractions in the region?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in attractions. For instance, after a run, you could explore the charming town of Padrón, home to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1004128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pedrón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2794030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Padrón Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes are conveniently located near these points of interest, allowing for a varied day out.
tips: This bridge of Roman origin rests on thirteen stone arches and passes over the Ulla river, considered the natural border between Pontevedra and La Coruña. Although several restoration works have been carried out, the construction of the bridge is between the year 25 a. C. and the 1st century AD. c.
Recreational park located at the confluence of the mouth of the Sar river in Ulla. The green zone of the peninsula contrasts with the Finsa wood products factory
